title: What businesses in Boston are worth waiting in line for?
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Brandy Melville on Newbury Street attracts long lines for its unique 'one size fits all' clothing during all weather conditions.
Blank Street coffee shop in Boston requires line security, highlighting its popularity despite some considering the wait a dealbreaker.
Brick Street Bagels in South Boston boasts long lines due to its strong online following, enticing locals and tourists alike.

title: Mass. woman pleads guilty to performing medical spa injections using counterfeit products
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Rebecca Fadanelli of Massachusetts pleaded guilty to using counterfeit products for cosmetic injections at two medical spas.
Fadanelli employed fake Botox and dermal fillers imported from China and Brazil, earning over $1 million despite lacking a license.
She faces up to 20 years in prison for illegal imports and up to 10 years for selling counterfeit drugs and devices.

title: Man fatally shot by Boston police near Northeastern University campus identified
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Jacob Graves, 29, was fatally shot by Boston police near Northeastern University during a mental health crisis call.
Graves attacked officers and EMS with a 'sword-like weapon' after calling 911 fearing threats to his life.
The DA's office and Boston police are investigating the shooting; officers' identities remain undisclosed.

title: Celtics’ Brad Stevens addresses ‘tiresome’ college basketball rumors
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Brad Stevens affirmed he will remain with the Celtics, dismissing rumors linking him to college basketball jobs.
Stevens, Celtics' president of basketball operations, was rumored for UNC's job before they hired Mike Malone.
Stevens has been with the Celtics since 2013 and continues to focus on building a competitive team in Boston.

title: Man gets life sentence in 2024 killing of Boston father of 2
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Widmaer Lapaix, 29, was sentenced to life with parole eligibility after 15 years for the 2024 killing of Maceo Martin.
Lapaix was convicted of second-degree murder after a nine-day trial for shooting Martin outside a Cambridge apartment.
Martin was shot during a dispute outside his ex-wife's home; he was pronounced dead at Boston Medical Center.

title: Lindsay Clancy will admit to killing her children if trial is split into 2 parts, defense says
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Lindsay Clancy's defense requests a split trial, admitting to killing her three children if granted.
Judge William Sullivan denied Clancy's request for a two-part trial on the grounds of overlapping issues.
Clancy's trial is set for July 20; her defense plans to pursue an insanity plea if no split trial.

title: Patriots trade Marte Mapu to Houston Texans
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Patriots trade linebacker Marte Mapu to Houston Texans for a 2027 sixth-round draft pick.
Mapu, initially drafted in 2023, played 17 games last season mainly on special teams.
Trade frees a roster spot and $1.5 million in cap space for New England Patriots.

title: Suspicious person prompts lockdown, canceled classes at R.I. college
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Community College of Rhode Island's Warwick campus was locked down due to a report of a suspicious man with a suspected weapon.
The suspect was seen entering woods near the campus with what appeared to be a firearm, prompting a police search.
Law enforcement located a backpack with an airsoft gun and black pellet rifle; the suspect remains unidentified.

title: Red Sox get more bad news after Triston Casas suffers setback in his recovery
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Triston Casas' recovery from a knee injury has stalled due to a rib muscle strain, delaying his return to the Red Sox.
Casas, who has played only 92 games since 2024, will undergo imaging after a setback in his rehabilitation attempts.
The Red Sox, who acquired Willson Contreras as a replacement, continue to wait for Casas to recover and boost their lineup.

title: What to know about the ‘massive’ military bunker beneath Trump’s ballroom
author: New York Times Service
bullets: The Presidential Emergency Operations Center is being rebuilt into a larger underground military facility beneath Trump's ballroom at the White House.
President Donald Trump claims the new facility will include bomb shelters, medical facilities, and defense against bioweapons.
A federal judge halted the ballroom construction, citing lack of Congressional approval, but allowed security-related work to continue.
